posts.content: Bochum und Tsukuba arbeiten an vielen gemeinsamen Initiativen, wie der sozialen Umsetzung von Forschungsergebnissen, industrieller Entwicklung und nachhaltiger Stadtentwicklung. Im Rahmen der gemeinsamen Zusammenarbeit haben Oberbürgermeister Thomas Eiskirch und Oberbürgermeister Tatsuo Igarashi heute im Rathaus von Tsukuba ein Memorandum of Understanding unterzeichnet, um den wirtschaftlichen Austausch zwischen Start-up-Unternehmen in beiden Städten zu fördern. Start-ups aus Bochum und Tsukuba profitieren von der Städtepartnerschaft: Beispielsweise erhalten Start-ups beider Städte beim Besuch der Partnerstadt eine Woche lang kostenlosen Zugang zu Co-Working Spaces. Beide Städte unterstützen zudem bei der Vernetzung mit dem lokalen Start-up-Ökosystem und stellen regelmäßig Infos zu Trends und Events bereit. „Die Partnerschaft mit Tsukuba zeigt, wie internationaler Austausch Innovation fördert. Start-ups aus Bochum erhalten neue Impulse, wertvolle Kontakte und die Chance, sich global zu vernetzen – das ist gelebte Zukunftsstadt,“ so Oberbürgermeister Thomas Eiskirch. Übrigens: Nicht nur die Architektur des Rathauses in Tsukuba ist hochmodern. Es spielt auch eine zentrale Rolle in der Smart City Entwicklung der Stadt. Mit Fokus auf digitale Verwaltung und Bürgerbeteiligung ist es ein Vorbild für innovative Stadtplanung. Auch wir haben in den letzten Jahren verstärkt in die digitale Transformation investiert, um den Bürger*innen einen einfacheren Zugang zu Verwaltungsdiensten zu ermöglichen: Bochum belegt aktuell Platz 4 im Bitkom Smart City Index der digitalsten deutschen Großstädte. Über 400 Dienstleistungen können mittlerweile online erledigt werden. Zudem hat unsere Smart City Innovation Unit Bochum aktuell über 100 Projekte in der Umsetzung, z.B. unsere Bochum App und die Dokumentenausgabebox. hashtag #Bochum hashtag #BochumGoesTsukuba hashtag #Tsukuba hashtag #Partnerstadt Bochum Economic Development Ruhr University Bochum …more

🇯🇵 Great to be flying together with the delegation to the twin city of Bochum „Tsukuba„ today! ✈️ 💬 Plenty of good conversations along the way — both at airport and during a few “walks” through the plane. Some truly interesting encounters and inspiring exchanges - and the trip just started. 🌱 Happy to be back on the ground now — and looking forward to our dinner tonight 🍽️ and moreover to the upcoming program at Tsukuba. The members of the delegation Thomas Eiskrich Lisa Methling Jagoda Josch Kirsten Ilk Christian Kalisch Sebastian Pewny Karsten Herlitz Mehriban Özdogan Léon Beck Martin Paul Maike Luhmann Marie Lilienfeld-Toal Heike Köckler Michael Vogelsang Dr. Mandana Banedj-Schafii Joerg Holstein Andreas Kluy Anuschka Fritzsche …more

Joining our Conference on Global Health Innovations: A great opportunity to showcase our interaction with our Japanese partners at University of Tsukuba. For more information and registration see: https://lnkd.in/e6YYUQYt . The conference aims to provide insights into the innovative healthcare ecosystem of Germany and Japan with a focus on @North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW) and the Stadt Bochum (twin city of Tsukuba) as well as Tokyo and Tsukuba. Bochum ha established a successful Health Campus that is a leading hub for healthcare, digital health and medical technology in Europe with a holistic, human-centered approach. The networking activities within the Health Campus community are managed by the HealthCampus Agency Bochum (www.gc-bo.de), a special task unit in close collaboration with Bochum Economic Development. The campus is embedded in the academic landscape of the Ruhr University Bochum (RUB) with its pre-clinical and translational/clinical facilities and university hospitals @ Universitätsklinikum der Ruhr-Universität Bochum (UKRUB) combining “research-based learning” with the most innovative patient-oriented cutting-edge medicine. Health professionals in science, the care sector as well as industry find a network of experts in NRW and Bochum to collaborate in solving current and future challenges in healthcare using a comprehensive, transdisciplinary approach with different partners such as Hochschule Bochum At the conference, speakers from industry and academia will provide insights into translational research, applied healthcare, health technologies and Global Health challenges. The event aims to connect German and Japanese entrepreneurs, industry representatives and researchers in health and healthcare by providing 1-on-1 meetings and networking opportunities next to insightful discussions and poster presentation sessions. …more

🌟 Cobalt presence effect on creep deformation of Ni-base single crystal superalloys !! 🌟 We are pleased to share our latest publication in Journal of Alloys and Compounds, focusing on Ni-base single-crystal superalloys — critical materials for jet engine components ✈️ . While Cobalt (Co) is a major alloying element in these materials, its specific role in deformation behaviour, especially in relation to crystal defects, has remained unclear. This study investigates how the presence of Co influences creep deformation at 800 °C under various stress conditions, with an emphasis on microstructural mechanisms, using Co-containing alloy and Co-free alloy. 🔍 Key findings: ✅Co facilitates primary creep: Co containing alloy shows greater primary creep strain, accompanied by single-slip if dislocations with stacking faults. ✅Co suppresses sudden failure: Co presence helps moderate sudden fracture via deformation twinning and dynamic recrystallization, which can otherwise lead to crack propagation. These insights highlight how Co not only affects the early-stage deformation but also plays a critical role in the final fracture behaviour — a key consideration for alloy design in high-performance turbine applications. 🧾 Check the article! Full article with subscription: https://lnkd.in/g53Ydp9K Preprint: https://lnkd.in/gry4KPuF …more

🌍❤️ I’m excited to announce that Bochum Economic Development will be co-hosting the "Conference on Global Health Innovations" alongside our esteemed partners in hashtag #Tokyo. Japan is known for its cutting-edge technology and medical expertise, making it the perfect place to discuss the future of Smart Health. Together, we will explore the healthcare ecosystems of hashtag #Japan and hashtag #Germany and discover how advanced technologies such as telemedicine, mobile health, artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), big data and robotics are transforming personalized medicine and global healthcare. 📍 April 24, 2025 📍 University of Tsukuba Tokyo-Campus 🙌 Join us for inspiring discussions, 1-on-1 networking and exciting collaborations! Participation is free! Please register here: https://lnkd.in/eVHWp58V NRW.Global Business DWIH Tokyo (German Centre for Research and Innovation Tokyo) University of Tsukuba Bochum Economic Development Ruhr University Bochum Stadt Bochum …more

Good things take time… ⏳💭 … and they are worth working on🌟! I am very happy to share our latest publication on low angle grain boundaries and re-segregation in single crystal Ni-base superalloys. It was great to work again on the topic of my PhD thesis together with great colleagues from the former CRC/TR 103. 🙏🙏 Many thanks to Alireza B. Parsa, Pascal Thome, Junyang He, Niels Jöns, Baptiste Gault, Gunther Eggeler and Jan Frenzel! 👉 Our article is now available here: https://lnkd.in/evDTuaKG hashtag #SFBTR103 hashtag #materialsscience hashtag #superalloys …more

On 24th of January, 2025, we held the presentation event of RUB-UT Metropolitan Research Summer School 2024 and theTsukuba Exchange Meeting, together with Prof. Uta Hohn from the Institute of Geography, Ruhr University Bochum! At the Exchange Meeting, about 30 people participated, including participants of the Summer School, as well as students from our university studying abroad at partner universities in Germany, and students from the Ruhr University Bochum who have studied at the University of Tsukuba or are planning to study there in the future, and deepened friendships! …more

This week at Ruhr University Bochum we have welcomed a delegation from our Japanese partner University of Tsukuba 筑波大学 (University of Tsukuba) on many special occasions: ✅ Jointly we raised our glass by invitation of the Japanese Consulate General to my colleague Uta Hohn who received special acknowledgement by the Japanese Government for her achievements in German-Japanese academic exchange, both in her research field of urban planning as well as between our two countries and our two institutions. ✅ We celebrated the start of operations of U Tsukuba’s overseas branch office on our campus ground and welcomed its new head of office Yusaku Nishijima in Stadt Bochum, which also happens to be twin city of Tsukuba. ✅ To kick off our joint conference on “Cutting Edge Research for Smart Societies” featuring prominent researchers and young scientists of our universities eager to cooperate on topics of sustainability, digitalization and artificial intelligence.
